reviews.review_msg: OMG,this course is excellent for boosting up your GPAs.2 take-home mids, one's short-answer and the other one's essay.I think the course is not organized and lectures are unhelpful.You DON'T need to come to class to get a very good mark. Almost everyone gets A+. What the HELL...? Maybe because it's summer term...bonus for hard working students ^^
Never made any sense in lecture and seems like using the lecture time to do his own studying. Tests seemed straightforward, but it ruins everything with his high grading system.
Doesn't seem to like to answer questions, or have the patience for those who ask them.  Very easy tests, but that's offset by the very high grading scale.

Redundant (3 hours to say what a better prof could more clearly articulate in 1); notes: paragraphs, often from the text; open-book midterms: good if don't mind paying $500 to reword the text; final exam: too open-ended, but gave extra time; helpful in office hrs. Overall, good for GPA, but complicates and makes tedious an otherwise simple course.
He is the worst prof I had at SFU. Can't believe he is still teaching!!!
it's an interesting course but the take home exams are not easy

If you study his notes and understand the textbook well, you will get an A in his class. Well organized lectures but boring sometimes. Overall, he is an enthusiastic teacher. Negatives: He doesn't know how to explain stuff in an easily understandable way, so you better figure it out from his helpful notes and the informative textbook.
